heading: FIELD MEMO · OPERATION SUNDOWN
body: The best operatives are the ones nobody ever suspects — primarily because they look exactly as confused as everyone else.

heading: CLASSIFIED BULLETIN 7-G
body: A good cover story is indistinguishable from the truth. A great cover story is the truth, rearranged slightly.

heading: SECURE TRANSMISSION — EYES ONLY
body: If you must blend in, remember: the loudest person in the room is never the one anyone's watching.

heading: AGENT BRIEFING · SECTION 4
body: The difference between a spy and an idiot is that the spy intended to do that.

heading: INTERNAL MEMO — DO NOT DISTRIBUTE
body: Plausible deniability begins with a convincing shrug.

heading: DIRECTIVE 9 · COUNTERINTELLIGENCE
body: When in doubt, act natural. When acting natural, stop acting natural — it's making you look suspicious.

heading: EYES ONLY — BURN AFTER READING
body: Three can keep a secret if two of them have no idea there is a secret.

heading: OPERATIONAL SECURITY NOTICE
body: Always assume you're being watched. Smile anyway. Let them wonder.

heading: FIELD REPORT — ASSET SUNFLOWER
body: The most effective disguise is unshakeable confidence and a completely unremarkable Tuesday.

heading: MEMO: BEHAVIORAL ANALYSIS UNIT
body: A nervous person and a bored person look nearly identical from across a crowded room. Use this.

heading: CLASSIFIED: LEVEL OMEGA
body: The mission brief said subtle. The debrief will determine how that went.

heading: DIRECTIVE 12 · SOCIAL OPERATIONS
body: People will believe almost anything if you say it in the same tone you use to order coffee.

heading: SECURE CHANNEL — RELAY BRAVO
body: The key to not looking guilty is genuinely not knowing what you did. Unfortunately, that's the hard part.

heading: AGENT HANDBOOK · APPENDIX C
body: Amateur spies worry about being caught. Professional spies worry about catching themselves.

heading: COUNTERINTEL DISPATCH 41-F
body: Surveillance footage is only a problem if they know what they're looking for. Act accordingly.

heading: INTERNAL COMM — STATION ECHO
body: Every double agent started out as someone who just seemed a little too agreeable.

heading: FIELD NOTE · OPERATION KETTLEDRUM
body: A convincing lie requires knowing the truth first. We recommend starting there.

heading: PRIORITY MEMO — DIRECTOR'S DESK
body: This agency has never, officially, existed. Thank you for your continued nonexistent service.

heading: MISSION PARAMETERS — UPDATE 3
body: Objective: complete the mission. Secondary objective: remember what the mission was.

heading: SECURE BRIEF · ASSET PROTOCOL
body: The word coincidence is not in our vocabulary. Everything is a signal. Especially that.

heading: FIELD MANUAL · CHAPTER 7
body: When caught doing something suspicious, the correct response is: "Oh, this? I do this all the time."

heading: CLEARANCE LEVEL: ABOVE YOUR PAY GRADE
body: The mission must be completed without arousing suspicion. No further guidance will be provided.

heading: OPERATIONAL NOTE — EYES ONLY
body: The Witness sees everything. The Crowd suspects nothing. The Agent hopes for the best. Good luck.

heading: DISPATCH FROM HQ · UNIT ZETA
body: Remember: ordinary people do ordinary things, ordinarily. Be ordinary. Extraordinarily ordinary.

heading: ASSET BRIEFING — REDACTED
body: Your mission, should you choose to accept it — and you should, because we already told everyone you did.
